final_text:
**RANInvestigator — verdict**

**Root cause is transport, not local RAN.**  
Evidence chain: repeated **MAJOR BACKHAUL_FLAP** on **LINK-BH-GNB-SYD-02**, link terminates on **GNB-SYD-02 ↔ CORE-AU-5GC**, and cell KPIs show **synchronous throughput collapses without PRB congestion**.

### Evidence summary

| Domain | Evidence | Read |
|---|---|---|
| Alarms | `query_alerts`: repeated `BACKHAUL_FLAP` on `LINK-BH-GNB-SYD-02` at 08:30, 08:45, 09:00, 09:15, 09:30 | Intermittent, transport-scoped, matches symptom periodicity |
| Topology | `query_graph`: `LINK-BH-GNB-SYD-02` connects `GNB-SYD-02` to `CORE-AU-5GC` | Fault sits on N3/backhaul path upstream of all cells |
| Blast radius | `query_graph`: `GNB-SYD-02` hosts **2 DUs / 6 cells** across both DUs | Single shared dependency explains all-cell impact |
| Cell KPIs | `query_telemetry`: all recent cells healthy at 09:45–09:55 with PRB ~40–57%, RRC ~99%, throughput normal | No persistent radio saturation or DU collapse |
| Intermittency pattern | `query_telemetry` on `CELL-SYD-02-1-1`: low throughput at 09:00/09:10/09:20/09:30/09:40 while PRB stays ~51–60% | Throughput falls without load spike; points away from air-interface congestion |

### Key KPI pattern

| Time | Cell | PRBUtil% | RRCSuccess% | DL Throughput Mbps | Read |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| 09:00 | CELL-SYD-02-1-1 | 51.1 | 94.23 | 77.3 | Throughput collapse, PRB normal |
| 09:10 | CELL-SYD-02-1-1 | 50.8 | 94.03 | 69.3 | Repeats at flap window |
| 09:20 | CELL-SYD-02-1-1 | 59.9 | 94.23 | 55.8 | Not congestion-driven |
| 09:30 | CELL-SYD-02-1-1 | 52.6 | 94.37 | 69.5 | Aligns with alarm at 09:30 |
| 09:40 | CELL-SYD-02-1-1 | 51.0 | 94.47 | 89.9 | Continued intermittent degradation |
| 09:45–09:55 | Multiple cells | 40–57 | 98.5–99.9 | 185–357 | Recovery across all cells |

### Why this is **not** a RAN-origin fault

- No evidence of **PRB congestion**: PRB remains moderate, not >85/90%.
- No DU/cell-specific alarm cluster: alarm stream is dominated by the **backhaul link**, not RRC/PRB/DU overload alarms on SYD-02.
- Cross-cell, cross-DU impact is simultaneous: classic signature of **shared transport impairment**.
- KPI recovery is broad and fast once flap clears: consistent with path instability, not radio tuning/hardware degradation.

### Localization

| Layer | Status |
|---|---|
| Radio cells | Secondary impact only |
| DUs / CU | No direct fault evidence |
| Shared transport | **Primary fault domain** |
| Fault object | **LINK-BH-GNB-SYD-02 (N3 backhaul)** |

### Operational assessment

- **Impact scope:** all 6 cells under `GNB-SYD-02`, both DUs.
- **User effect:** intermittent throughput loss; some transient session/RRC instability likely when backhaul flaps.
- **Most probable mechanism:** N3 backhaul path flap causing temporary packet loss/reconvergence and throughput collapse at gNB egress.

### Immediate actions

1. Treat as **transport incident**; assign to backhaul/IP/MPLS team.
2. Check carrier/optics and upstream path for `LINK-BH-GNB-SYD-02`: interface errors, LOS/LOF, flap counters, routing reconvergence.
3. Correlate with core-facing port and any LAG/member instability on the N3 path.
4. Keep RAN on watch only; no radio retune or capacity action indicated from current evidence.

**Bottom line:** `GNB-SYD-02` throughput loss is **transport-rooted on backhaul LINK-BH-GNB-SYD-02**, not a RAN congestion or DU/cell fault.
